criterion: Demonstrates the full menu flow without errors and without duplicate menu instances after Play, Pause, Resume, Restart, and Exit.

forms.assessment_process: Run the game and execute Play, Pause, Resume, Pause, Restart, then Exit, and confirm no errors and only one menu instance exists.
forms.explanation: End to end flow catches integration bugs like duplicated UI, stuck pause state, or broken scene transitions that unit checks miss.

criterion: Defines the gameplay scene path once in menu.gd as a constant or exported variable used by Play and Restart.

forms.assessment_process: Open menu.gd and verify a single path variable exists and both Play and Restart reference it rather than inline strings.
forms.explanation: Centralizing the scene path prevents brittle logic when files are renamed and reduces maintenance errors.

criterion: Uses one menu.tscn and one menu.gd to support main and pause modes via a mode flag that controls visible buttons.

forms.assessment_process: Inspect the project for a single menu scene and script, then verify a mode flag toggles Play versus Resume and Restart visibility.
forms.explanation: A single implementation reduces duplicated logic and prevents drift between main menu and pause menu behavior.

criterion: Preserves the remaining power up duration across pause and resume, so the timer continues from the same remaining time.

forms.assessment_process: Activate a power up, note remaining time, pause for several seconds, resume, and confirm remaining time matches the pre pause value.
forms.explanation: Timer correctness is a core gameplay expectation and prevents exploits or broken power up behavior when pausing.

criterion: Sets the menu to process while paused, so its buttons remain interactive when the scene tree is paused.

forms.assessment_process: Pause the game and verify the menu still receives input and button clicks while gameplay nodes stop updating.
forms.explanation: Pause menus must remain usable during a paused scene, otherwise the player can get stuck.

criterion: Uses SceneTree.paused to stop gameplay updates while the pause menu is open, and gameplay resumes when unpaused.

forms.assessment_process: Open the pause menu and confirm SceneTree.paused becomes true and movement and timers stop, then resume and confirm they continue.
forms.explanation: Using the engine pause mechanism ensures consistent freezing across nodes and avoids partial pause bugs.

criterion: Resets the session on Restart, including score, player spawn, enemy positions, and collectible availability.

forms.assessment_process: Start a run, change score and positions, collect items, then press Restart and verify all listed state returns to initial values.
forms.explanation: Restart must reliably return the game to a known baseline to avoid soft locks and inconsistent progression.

criterion: Resumes the game when Escape is pressed while the pause menu is open, matching the Resume button behavior.

forms.assessment_process: Open the pause menu, press Escape, and confirm the menu closes and the game unpauses identically to clicking Resume.
forms.explanation: Escape to resume is a common usability expectation and prevents input inconsistency between keyboard and UI.

criterion: Restores gameplay on Resume without changing game state, including player position, score, and active power up status.

forms.assessment_process: Pause mid action, record position and score, resume, and confirm values match and gameplay continues from the same moment.
forms.explanation: Resume must be non destructive so pausing does not alter outcomes or break player trust.

criterion: Displays Resume, Restart, and Exit in pause mode, and hides the Play button in this mode.

forms.assessment_process: Open the pause menu during gameplay and verify only Resume, Restart, and Exit are visible and Play is not shown.
forms.explanation: Correct button set prevents confusing actions and ensures the menu matches the current context.

criterion: Pauses gameplay and shows the pause menu overlay when Escape is pressed, without reloading or replacing the gameplay scene.

forms.assessment_process: During gameplay press Escape and confirm the current scene instance remains and the menu appears over it while the game pauses.
forms.explanation: Overlay pause avoids losing session state and prevents unintended restarts or scene churn.

criterion: Closes the application when Exit is clicked from either menu mode, without errors in the debugger output.

forms.assessment_process: Click Exit from main menu and pause menu in separate runs and confirm the app terminates and no errors appear in output.
forms.explanation: Clean shutdown prevents stuck processes and is a baseline quality expectation for shipped builds.

criterion: Loads the gameplay scene on Play and removes the main menu from view, with no errors and no menu UI left on screen.

forms.assessment_process: Launch, click Play, and verify the gameplay scene is active and the menu is gone, then check output for errors.
forms.explanation: Scene transition correctness is essential to start gameplay cleanly and avoid UI stacking bugs.

criterion: Displays only Play and Exit in main menu mode, and centers the menu container on screen.

forms.assessment_process: Launch to main menu and verify only two buttons exist and the menu container is visually centered in the viewport.
forms.explanation: A minimal, centered main menu meets usability expectations and avoids confusing extra controls.

criterion: Sets menu.tscn as the main scene, so launch shows the main menu first and gameplay does not start until Play is clicked.

forms.assessment_process: Check Project Settings run main scene points to menu.tscn, then run and confirm gameplay is not active until Play.
forms.explanation: Correct entry point ensures consistent startup behavior and prevents unintended gameplay running behind the menu.
